results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP AN INTEGRATED TREATMENT SYSTEM THAT COUPLES NOVEL SORBENTS WITH OXYGEN NANOBUBBLES TO ACHIEVE PHOSPHONATE DEGRADATION AND PHOSPHATE ADSORPTION SIMULTANEOUSLY IN ORDER TO CAPTURE PHOSPHOROUS FROM WASTEWATER AND REUSE AS A SLOW-RELEASE FERTILIZER. ACTIVITIES:THE PROJECT WILL (1) DESIGN AND DEVELOP ZIRCONIUM-MODIFIED MICA MINERALS TO BE COUPLED WITH OXYGEN NANOBUBBLES, (2) ASSESS THE ADSORPTION AND DEGRADATION EFFICACY OF PHOSPHONATE ON THE ZIRCONIUM-MODIFIED MICA MINERAL AND OXYGEN NANOBUBBLE SYSTEM, AND (3) ASSESS THE ATTRIBUTES OF PHOSPHOROUS-LADEN MICA MINERALS AS SLOW-RELEASE FERTILIZER.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS, AS WELL AS PEER REVIEWED JOURNAL ARTICLES ON AN INNOVATIVE, INTEGRATED SYSTEM FOR REMOVAL, RECOVERY, AND REUSE OF PHOSPHOROUS RESOURCES FROM WASTEWATER. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THE PROJECT INCLUDE WATER TREATMENT UTILITIES, NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGERS, AND COMMUNITIES AFFECTED BY EUTROPHICATION.
